title: Dungeon Config
description: Configuration for the pi-dungeon sandbox extension. Used at ~/.pi/agent/dungeon.json (global) and <project>/.pi/dungeon.json (per-project).

allowedHosts.description: Hostnames the sandbox is allowed to reach over HTTPS. Supports wildcards (e.g. "*.github.com"). Global and per-project lists are concatenated.

secrets.description: Secrets injected into matching HTTP requests via placeholder replacement. Each key is a secret name (e.g. GH_TOKEN). The guest references it as $GH_TOKEN in headers. Global and per-project are merged; per-project wins on key conflict.

keychain.description: macOS Keychain account name under service "pi-dungeon".

hosts.description: Hosts that receive this secret value.

mounts.description: Additional host directories mounted into the VM. Each entry is a host path, optionally suffixed with ':ro' or ':rw' to set the mount mode (default: ro). The host path is expanded via ~ and used as both host and guest path. Example: "~/projects/work/foo" or "~/projects/work/bar:rw". Global and per-project lists are concatenated.

hiddenPaths.description: Workspace paths completely hidden from the guest. Useful for secret files like .env. Paths are relative to workspace root (prefix with /).

tmpfsPaths.description: Workspace paths shadowed from host with guest-writable tmpfs overlay. Useful for dependency directories like node_modules or .venv that should be isolated. Paths are relative to workspace root (prefix with /).

env.description: Environment variables set inside the dungeon VM. Global and per-project are merged; per-project wins on key conflict.

resources.description: VM resource limits. Per-project overrides global field-by-field.

memory.description: VM memory size in qemu syntax (e.g. "1G", "2G", "512M"). Default: "1G".
